items.title: Ships divert course mid-trip as global contest for oil heats up

children.content: Australia is stitching together a steady supply of fuel through a patchwork of relationships. But experts question how long this new chain can last.

articles.title: Vance warns Iran not to 'play' US ahead of Pakistan peace talks

children.content: The centre of Islamabad has been locked down as US delegates, including JD Vance, travel to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran.

image.altText: A man puts his hand in front of the camera as he speaks to reporters.

articles.title: Australia and Singapore aim to meet fuel supply needs as crisis continues

children.content: Australia and Singapore have agreed to make "maximum efforts" to meet each other's fuel and gas needs as the crisis in the Middle East continues, with the prime minister securing an encouraging but not legally binding deal.

image.altText: Twomen in suites walk through a door with bright sunlight behind them.

image.altText: A man stands near a damaged building from which piles of rubble spill to the ground

items.title: 'Sounds good to me': How Trump was sold war in Iran

children.content: Extraordinary reporting reveals how Israel made the case for regime change in Iran — and how the fallout is driving a profound shift in American opinion, amid deep uncertainty about what the war has achieved.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu shake hands.

items.title: Iran's grip on Strait of Hormuz 'not part of our planning', Israel ambassador says

items.title: Behind Canberra's political veil, the mood is grim

children.content: This is not over. The hard stuff is only just beginning. And it's going to get bumpy, testing the nation's resilience and its political leadership.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese wears a suit and blue tie, and black-rimmed glasses. He stares intently just past the camera.

items.title: A look at the sea mines Iran claims are in the Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Iran claims to have littered a central shipping passage in the Strait of Hormuz with sea mines, in a move analysts say could be a "coercive threat" to maintain its grip on the crucial waterway.

image.altText: A smartphone screen shows traffic in Strait of Hormuz.
